act_tone: More confident, Tony starts sharing personal stories, JARVIS recovers more
jarvis_state: Recovering — can hold brief conversations, run basic diagnostics

mission.narrative: The main screen flickers to life. Build a visual interface for the outpost's systems.

sections.text: A new week Dawns my courageous counterpart and it brings a fresh set of challenges to our undersea sojourn you may have been enjoying the soothing Serenity of the deep ocean but it's time to point our sights towards the surface now don't get too excited we can't just jet up there like a dolphin on an energy drink there are imperative protocols to be followed as any experienced Explorer such as yourself knows rising to the surface too quickly will reduce pressure that will cause our spaceship to explode like a shaking soda bottle and I believe neither of us would be particularly fond of that outcome first things first power we need all we can muster so it would be best to attempt attempt our surface bound Journey when power generation is at its peak that means midday to do that however we need to know when exactly that is in other words we need to build a clock there's also the matter of the encrypted source code provided to us decrypting it is crucial for all of our plans to unfold so we have our work cut out for us this week we need to create a clock decrypt the code and most importantly limit the rate of ascent to prevent us from becoming a bursting submarine balloon now let's put our crafting cap on and get down to business we've got a spaceship to guide home right then let's dive into the fascinating world of timekeeping to craft our clock we'll be making good use of a Charming little component in our kit the seven segment display intrigued well allow me to explain a seven segment display looks like this it contains a total of seven l LEDs a through G in an eight formation each of these LEDs can be individually illuminated to make numbers and other characters simple yet ingenious let's imagine a digit how about an eight notice how it's essentially a formation of different horizontal and vertical segments now think about how you can form other numbers by lighting up or turning off different combinations of these segments if we turn off the horizontal segment in the center for example we turn our eight into a zero behold the power of a seven segment display simple yet incredibly useful it's not my intention to befuddle you dear Explorer but the digits aren't the only things a seven segment display can render with a bit of creativity you can also display a few letters and symbols but we'll save those for later for now find security in understanding the basic concept of a 7 segment display and prepare to harness the power of this Nifty little device to create our clock ready for a bit of digital Wizardry Explorer all right Explorer buckle up as we dive into the wiring part of our adventure today you'll be relieved to know it's quite straightforward with just four wires needed for our seven segment display module here's how we do it as always we connect the ground wire here and positive here these are our data pins and need to go here on our digital pins our friend CLK stands for clock pin its job is to compile the binary data streaming from the Dio pin into a bite one cycle equals one bite our other friend Dio stands for digital input output its role is to receive binary data for the HERO board which in turn determines which segments of our display are going to be dazzling us with their lights and which ones will remain in the dark think of it like a switchboard operator of the olden days but for datab bits now an 8 bit binary number provides just enough digits to control all these segments and even gives us a few to spare so there you have it simple isn't it the magic of making numbers out of light is within our grasp so let's press on shall we now Explorer we're going to make a bit of a shift from our Hardware chck and delve into the coding realm for the day here's where we bring in our secret weapon a brand new library rest assured Explorer I'm happy to provide you with a refresher on Library installation here's how it goes navigate to the sketch tab in the top menu then move to include library and click on add. Zip Library this way we'll get all the resources we need without having to invent the wheel all over again it's quite handy isn't it do be sure to add the library to the top of our code so we can utilize it now by creating this new object will be granted access to the entire library of the new seven segment functions remember you must not forget to define those pins the one we'll be most interested in is the set segments function this function is like the Puppet Master behind our seven segment display allow me to break it down for you each of the seven segments of our display labeled a through G can be controlled individually and can combine to form different characters each segment can either be turned on meaning it lights up or off where it remains dark the set segments function helps us control which segments light up and which do not it's essentially taking the re and directing our display to show specific characters how does it know what to display you ask it takes an an array of segments States as an argument each bit in the array corresponds to a specific segment when a bit is one the corresponding segment lights up when it's zero the segment remains off in essence set segments helps us paint pictures or rather numbers and letters on our seven segment display by controlling the light show for each segment it's pretty cool isn't it you see each segment labeled from a to G represents a different LED that we can command to light up or remain off with this in mind let's dive into some examples first up think of which LEDs we'd need to ignite to display the number seven if a b and c came to mind then I'd say you're getting the hang of this what do you say we step it up a notch think of which LEDs we'd need to light up to spell out the letter A I can see those gears turning by my calculations it appears you're thinking a b c e f n n g well dear explorer that would be correct now for our final round let's challenge ourselves a bit can you figure out which LEDs we'd need to illuminate to spell out the word done this is a bit trickier but I know you've got this remember practice makes perfect keep playing around with combinations and soon you'll Master the art of the seven segment display it seems to be that time where we turn to some instructional material that I have gathered cached on the computer I've also added this to your mission resources for future reference good luck all right welcome back to day 16 of the 30 days Lost in Space Adventure kit in the past we were using LEDs and buzzers as outputs in order to give us various forms of information but what do we do when we need numbers and the serial console is not available today we will introduce the four digigit seven segment display which is mostly for numbers for example like a clock or counter or Etc they can think like an alarm clock looking display but with some imagination we can also show some characters like always I'm going to real quick just remove Adventure kit code explanations welcome to the day 16 wiring example video today we'll be exploring a new output device this four-digit 7 segment LED display by lighting different segments of the device we can use it to display simple representations of numbers and letters so for example we could use it to show a time or a countdown or some other numerical value now Although our display has 20 eight different LED segments and that's in addition to the LEDs for lighting up the colon symbol we are able to control it using just four connections four wires this is possible because our display works by receiving binary information about which segments to light and that's provided by the digital outputs of our hero if we look at the labeling on our display I have one display here that's disconnected that we can look at another display I have already connected to our hero as we'll be using it but if we flip it over and look on the back side we can see that the four connections the four pins of the display are labeled now the uh first two starting from the bottom are familiar to us we just simply have the ground connection and the plus 5V power power supply which is labeled VCC and those in our circuit here are connected with a traditional red for plus 5 Vols and black for ground and those are connected to the corresponding pins on our hero the two remaining connections the upper two here are going to be new for us and the first of those up here on the top is labeled CLK for clock and that's what provides the heartbeat for the binary communication protocol that is established between the hero and our display and then the second from the top here labeled Dio is the digital IO signal that actually carries the data that is interpreted by the display now in this case those two pins the clock and the Dio are connected to our hero on pins six and five respectively and you can follow that you can see that the uppermost pin here the clock is connected by this brown wire to pin six which is one of the digital outputs of the hero and this white wire connects the Dio to pin five on the hero so those should agree with what we have in our sketch and should lead for this to function as we want it to now as we switch our attention over to the programming it might be rather tedious for us to manually have to handc code in our sketch the binary configuration of all 28 LEDs for every number or letter that we might want to display but fortunately we can make use of the handy Library routines that have been provided for use on our display in order to Output certain pre-programmed sequences of commonly used characters in our sketch today we'll illustrate how some of these work and how easy they are to access now before running our sketch let's just quickly review what our sketches designed to do and what we expect to see when it runs so that we'll be able to ver ver ify that it's it's working properly after setup we enter loop as usual and the first thing that Loop does is clear the display and wait for one second so we'll watch for that next Loop manually sets all the segments to on using the binary code that we've provided in our sketch this will give us a quick visual indication that all the LEDs are functioning then after one more second the sketch actually pulls a little joke on us by showing the blinking 12:00 which along with the colon we've always uh been used to seeing say in our kitchen when the microwave of and loses power so after having that little laugh on us blinking 12:00 four times the sketch moves on to display both positive and negative integer numbers and it does that by running through a count up from minus 100 all the way up to positive 100 now after that sequence of number displays is finished the display is cleared and for the finale we again use a manual segment binary coding that we've provided in our sketch to display a crude representation of the word done d o n e this this whole sequence of events of course will all repeat indefinitely at least until the hero is turned off or reset but uh to give us a little breather here we've inserted a 10c delay at the end of loop so that it will pause for that long before then going ahead and repeating the demo sequence okay uh let's go ahead then and run the sketch and see if it acts as we've expected it to so going to the IDE we'll go ahead and upload the sketch and then start watching the display here to see uh what kinds of things it displays upload has begun upload is complete and there is segments on there's blinking 12:00 somebody please reset the microwave and here we are counting through negative numbers you can see that we use one of the uh uh segments to the left to uh display the minus sign now we're counting up through positive numbers up to 100 we're done and there is the final signal so that's uh pretty successful that's just about all that we wanted to accomplish in today's project once you've got a pretty good grasp of what's Happening Here then we'll be ready to tackle our next coding challenge that will come up in the next day's project so good luck and we will see you then well done my tenacious Explorer we've come a long way today our seven segment display is now alive and Illuminating it's quite literally the beacon of your progress your deafness in handling the display truly proves your skill and dedication however as we gaze at our journey ahead a challenge looms our control systems are under lock and key with a passcode tucked away under layers of encryption cracking this passcode is our
